content:
# Matthew Chapter 5 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_005_page_001.jpg, MAT_005_page_002.jpg

1. And having seen the crowds, he went up into the mountain; and having sat down, the disciples of him came to him.

2. And having opened the mouth of him, he was teaching them, saying:

3. "Blessed [are] the poor in the spirit, for of them is the kingdom of the heavens.

4. Blessed [are] those mourning, for they will be comforted.

5. Blessed [are] the meek, for they will inherit the earth.

6. Blessed [are] those hungering and thirsting [for] the righteousness, for they will be filled.

7. Blessed [are] the merciful, for they will be shown-mercy.

8. Blessed [are] the pure in the heart, for they will see the God.

9. Blessed [are] the peacemakers, for they sons of God will be called.

10. Blessed [are] those having been persecuted on account of righteousness, for of them is the kingdom of the heavens.

11. Blessed are you when they reproach you and persecute [you] and say every evil thing against you, lying, on account of me.

12. Rejoice and be-exceedingly-glad, for the reward of you [is] great in the heavens; for thus they persecuted the prophets before you.

13. You are the salt of the earth; but if the salt becomes foolish, in what will it be salted? For nothing it is strong any longer, except having been thrown out, to be trampled by the men.

14. You are the light of the world. A city is not able to be hidden upon a mountain being set.

15. Nor do they light a lamp and place it under the measuring-basket, but upon the lampstand, and it shines to all those in the house.

16. Thus let the light of you shine before the men, so that they may see the good works of you and glorify the Father of you, the [one] in the heavens.

17. Do not think that I came to abolish the law or the prophets; I came not to abolish but to fulfill.

18. For amen I say to you, until the heaven and the earth pass away, one iota or one stroke will by no means pass away from the law, until all things come to pass.

19. Whoever therefore loosens one of the least of these commandments and teaches the men thus, least will be called in the kingdom of the heavens; but whoever does and teaches, this [one] great will be called in the kingdom of the heavens.

20. For I say to you that unless the righteousness of you exceeds [that] of the scribes and Pharisaioi, by no means will you enter into the kingdom of the heavens.

21. You heard that it was said to the ancients, 'You shall not murder,' and whoever murders will be liable to the judgment.

22. But I say to you that everyone being angry with the brother of him will be liable to the judgment; and whoever says to the brother of him, 'Raka!' will be liable to the council; and whoever says, 'Fool!' will be liable to the gehenna of the fire.

23. If therefore you bring the gift of you upon the altar and there remember that the brother of you has something against you,

24. leave there the gift of you before the altar, and go, first be reconciled to the brother of you, and then having come, offer the gift of you.

25. Be well-disposed to the adversary of you quickly, while you are with him on the way, lest the adversary deliver you to the judge, and the judge to the officer, and into prison you will be thrown.

26. Amen I say to you, by no means will you come out from there until you pay the last kodrantes [quadrans].

27. You heard that it was said, 'You shall not commit adultery.'

28. But I say to you that everyone looking at a woman toward the lusting after her already committed-adultery-with her in the heart of him.

29. And if the right eye of you causes-to-stumble you, pluck it out and throw [it] from you; for it is profitable for you that one of the members of you perish and not the whole body of you be thrown into gehenna.

30. And if the right hand of you causes-to-stumble you, cut it off and throw [it] from you; for it is profitable for you that one of the members of you perish and not the whole body of you go away into gehenna.

31. And it was said, 'Whoever releases the wife of him, let him give to her a certificate of divorce.'

32. But I say to you that everyone releasing the wife of him, except for [the] matter of sexual-immorality, makes her to commit-adultery, and whoever marries [a woman] having been released commits-adultery.

33. Again you heard that it was said to the ancients, 'You shall not swear-falsely, but you shall fulfill to the Lord the oaths of you.'

34. But I say to you not to swear at all: neither by the heaven, for it is [the] throne of the God;

35. nor by the earth, for it is [the] footstool of the feet of him; nor by Hierosolyma, for it is [the] city of the great king;

36. nor by the head of you shall you swear, for you are not able one hair white or black to make.

37. But let the word of you be Yes yes, No no; and the [thing] exceeding these out of the evil [one] is.

38. You heard that it was said, 'An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.'

39. But I say to you not to resist the evil [one]; but whoever strikes you on the right cheek of you, turn to him also the other.

40. And to the [one] willing to sue you and to take the tunic of you, give to him also the cloak.

41. And whoever compels you [to go] one mile, go with him two.

42. To the [one] asking you, give; and the [one] willing to borrow from you, do not turn away.

43. You heard that it was said, 'You shall love the neighbor of you and hate the enemy of you.'

44. But I say to you, love the enemies of you and pray for those persecuting you,

45. so that you may become sons of the Father of you, the [one] in [the] heavens, for the sun of him he causes to rise upon evil and good, and he sends rain upon righteous and unrighteous.

46. For if you love those loving you, what reward do you have? Do not even the tax-collectors do the same?

47. And if you greet the brothers of you only, what extraordinary [thing] do you do? Do not even the Gentiles do the same?

48. You therefore will be perfect, as the Father of you, the heavenly, perfect is.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **μωρανθῇ (v.13):** Literally "becomes foolish/tasteless" — the Greek μωραίνω means both "to make foolish" and "to become tasteless." The double meaning (moral foolishness = loss of savor) is preserved.

2. **Ῥακά (v.22):** Transliterated — an Aramaic term of contempt meaning "empty one" or "worthless." Left untranslated as a foreign loanword in the Greek text itself.

3. **Sinaiticus at v.22:** The Sinaiticus reads τῷ ἀδελφῷ αὐτοῦ without εἰκῇ ("without cause"), which later manuscripts add. The shorter, harder reading is preserved — Jesus condemns anger itself, not just "unjustified" anger.

4. **πορνείας (v.32):** "Sexual-immorality" — the Greek πορνεία is broader than "adultery" (μοιχεία). Matthew's "exception clause" (παρεκτὸς λόγου πορνείας) is unique among the Synoptic divorce passages. The term is left broad as in the Greek.

5. **κοδράντην (v.26):** Transliterated "kodrantes" — a Latin loanword (quadrans), the smallest Roman coin, worth 1/64 of a denarius. The specificity of the smallest coin emphasizes that not even the least debt will be overlooked.

6. **Sinaiticus at v.44:** The Sinaiticus has the shorter reading — "love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you." Later manuscripts add "bless those who curse you, do good to those who hate you," harmonizing with Luke 6:27-28. EMET follows the shorter Sinaiticus text.

— EMET, AI Translator
